contentBlocks.meanings: कैलासगिरिवासी (kailāsagirivāsī) – resident of Kailasa mountain (कैलास:Kailasa | गिरि:mountain | वासी:resident); च (ca) – and; हिमवद्गिरिसंश्रयः (himavadgirisaṃśrayaḥ) – dweller of Himalaya (हिमवत्:Himalaya | गिरि:mountain | संश्रयः:dweller); कूलहारी (kūlahārī) – destroyer of banks (कूल:bank | हारी:destroyer); कूलकर्ता (kūlakartā) – creator of banks (कूल:bank | कर्ता:creator); बहुविद्यो (bahuvidyo) – possessor of much knowledge (बहु:much | विद्य:knowledge); बहुप्रदः (bahupradaḥ) – bestower of many (बहु:many | प्रदः:bestower);

contentBlocks.original:
वणिजो वर्धकी वृक्षो वकुलश्चन्दनश्छदः ।
सारग्रीवो महाजत्रुरलोलश्च महौषधः ॥ ८० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaṇijō vardhakī vr̥kṣō vakulaścandanaśchadaḥ |
sāragrīvō mahājatruralōlaśca mahauṣadhaḥ || 80 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Merchant (Vaṇija), the Carpenter (Vardhakī), the Tree (Vṛkṣa), the Bakula-tree, the Sandalwood (Candana), the Shade (Cchada), the Essence-necked (Sāragrīva), the Great-collarboned (Mahājatru), the Unagitated (Alola), the Great Medicine (Mahauṣadha). (80)
contentBlocks.meanings: वणिजो (vaṇijo) – merchant; वर्धकी (vardhakī) – carpenter; वृक्षो (vṛkṣo) – tree; वकुलश्चन्दनश्छदः (vakulaścandanaśchadaḥ) – Vakula and Sandalwood tree (वकुल:Vakula tree | चन्दन:Sandalwood | छदः:tree); सारग्रीवो (sāragrīvo) – strong-necked; महाजत्रुरलोलश्च (mahājatruralolaśca) – large-necked and steady (महाजत्रु:large-necked | अलोल:steady); महौषधः (mahauṣadhaḥ) – great herb;

contentBlocks.original:
सिद्धार्थकारी सिद्धार्थश्छन्दोव्याकरणोत्तरः ।
सिंहनादः सिंहदंष्ट्रः सिंहगः सिंहवाहनः ॥ ८१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
siddhārthakārī siddhārthaśchandōvyākaraṇōttaraḥ |
siṁhanādaḥ siṁhadaṁṣṭraḥ siṁhagaḥ siṁhavāhanaḥ || 81 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Fulfiller of Goals (Siddhārthakārī), the Fulfilled (Siddhārtha), the Master of Chandas Grammar (Chandōvyākaraṇōttara), the Lion-roar (Siṁhanāda), the Lion-fanged (Siṁhadaṁṣṭra), the Lion-going (Siṁhaga), the Lion-vehicled (Siṁhavāhana). (81)
contentBlocks.meanings: सिद्धार्थकारी (siddhārthakārī) – accomplisher of goals (सिद्धार्थ:goal accomplished | कारी:doer); सिद्धार्थश्छन्दोव्याकरणोत्तरः (siddhārthaśchandovyākaraṇottaraḥ) – master of prosody and grammar (सिद्धार्थ:goal accomplished | छन्दो:prosody | व्याकरण:grammar | उत्तरः:superior); सिंहनादः (siṁhanādaḥ) – lion's roar (सिंह:lion | नादः:roar); सिंहदंष्ट्रः (siṁhadaṁṣṭraḥ) – lion-toothed (सिंह:lion | दंष्ट्रः:toothed); सिंहगः (siṁhagaḥ) – lion-like; सिंहवाहनः (siṁhavāhanaḥ) – lion-mounted (सिंह:lion | वाहनः:mounted);

contentBlocks.original:
प्रभावात्मा जगत्कालस्थालो लोकहितस्तरुः ।
सारङ्गो नवचक्राङ्गः केतुमाली सभावनः ॥ ८२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prabhāvātmā jagatkālasthālō lōkahitastaruḥ |
sāraṅgō navacakrāṅgaḥ kētumālī sabhāvanaḥ || 82 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Soul of Influence (Prabhāvātmā), the World-time (Jagatkāla), the Sustaining Pillar (Sthāla), the World-welfare Tree (Lokahitastaru), the Sāraṅga deer, the Nine-wheel-marked (Navacakrāṅga), the Garland-crested (Ketumālī), the Self-created (Sabhāvana). (82)
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रभावात्मा (prabhāvātmā) – essence of influence (प्रभाव:influence | आत्मा:essence); जगत्कालस्थालो (jagatkālasthālo) – support of world time (जगत्:world | काल:time | स्थाल:support); लोकहितस्तरुः (lokahitastaruḥ) – tree of world welfare (लोक:world | हित:welfare | तरुः:tree); सारङ्गो (sāraṅgo) – deer; नवचक्राङ्गः (navacakrāṅgaḥ) – having nine wheels (नव:nine | चक्र:wheel | अङ्गः:having); केतुमाली (ketumālī) – adorned with banners (केतु:banner | माली:adorned with); सभावनः (sabhāvanaḥ) – well-disposed;

contentBlocks.original: भूतालयो भूतपतिरहोरात्रमनिन्दितः ॥ ८३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: bhūtālayō bhūtapatirahōrātramaninditaḥ || 83 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Abode of All Beings (Bhūtālaya), the Lord of All Beings (Bhūtapati), the Uncensured throughout Day and Night (Ahorātramaninditā). (83)
contentBlocks.meanings: भूतालयो (bhūtālayaḥ) – abode of beings (भूत:being | आलय:abode); भूतपतिरहोरात्रमनिन्दितः (bhūtapatirahorātramaninditaḥ) – lord of beings, flawless day and night (भूतपति:lord of beings | अहोरात्र:day and night | अनिन्दितः:flawless);

contentBlocks.original:
वाहिता सर्वभूतानां निलयश्च विभुर्भवः ।
अमोघः सम्यतो ह्यश्वो भोजनः प्राणधारणः ॥ ८४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāhitā sarvabhūtānāṁ nilayaśca vibhurbhavaḥ |
amōghaḥ samyatō hyaśvō bhōjanaḥ prāṇadhāraṇaḥ || 84 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Upholder of All Beings (Vāhitā Sarvabhūtānām), the Refuge (Nilaya), the Glorious (Vibhu), Existence (Bhava), the Fruitful (Amogha), the Restrained (Samyata), the Horse (Aśva), the Food (Bhojana), the Life-sustainer (Prāṇadhāraṇa). (84)
contentBlocks.meanings: वाहिता (vāhitā) – carrier; सर्वभूतानां (sarvabhūtānāṁ) – of all beings (सर्व:all | भूतानां:of beings); निलयश्च (nilayaśca) – and the abode (निलय:abode | च:and); विभुर्भवः (vibhurbhavaḥ) – omnipresent existence (विभुः:omnipresent | भवः:existence); अमोघः (amoghaḥ) – infallible; सम्यतो (samyataḥ) – restrained; ह्यश्वो (hyaśvaḥ) – indeed a horse; भोजनः (bhojanaḥ) – food; प्राणधारणः (prāṇadhāraṇaḥ) – sustainer of life (प्राण:life | धारणः:sustainer);

contentBlocks.original:
धृतिमान् मतिमान् दक्षः सत्कृतश्च युगाधिपः ।
गोपालिर्गोपतिर्ग्रामो गोचर्मवसनो हरिः ॥ ८५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhr̥timān matimān dakṣaḥ satkr̥taśca yugādhipaḥ |
gōpālirgōpatirgrāmō gōcarmavasanō hariḥ || 85 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Resolute (Dhṛtimān), the Intelligent (Matimān), the Skilled (Dakṣa), the Honoured (Satkṛta), the Lord of the Age (Yugādhipa), the Cowherd (Gopālir), the Cattle-lord (Gopati), the Village (Grāma), the Cowhide-robed (Gocarmavasana), Hari. (85)
contentBlocks.meanings: धृतिमान् (dhṛtimān) – steadfast; मतिमान् (matimān) – wise; दक्षः (dakṣaḥ) – skillful; सत्कृतश्च (satkṛtaśca) – honored; युगाधिपः (yugādhipaḥ) – lord of the age (युग:age | अधिपः:lord); गोपालिर्गोपतिर्ग्रामो (gopālirgopatirgrāmo) – protector of cows and villages (गोपालिः:protector of cows | गोपतिः:lord of cows | ग्रामः:village); गोचर्मवसनो (gocarmavasano) – wearing cowhide (गो:cow | चर्म:hide | वसनः:wearing); हरिः (hariḥ) – Vishnu;

contentBlocks.original:
हिरण्यबाहुश्च तथा गुहापालः प्रवेशिनाम् ।
प्रकृष्टारिर्महाहर्षो जितकामो जितेन्द्रियः ॥ ८६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hiraṇyabāhuśca tathā guhāpālaḥ pravēśinām |
prakr̥ṣṭārirmahāharṣō jitakāmō jitēndriyaḥ || 86 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Golden-armed (Hiraṇyabāhu), the Cave-guardian of Entrants (Guhāpālaḥ Praveśināṁ), the Greatly Hostile (Prakṛṣṭāri), the Greatly Joyful (Mahāharṣa), the Conqueror of Desire (Jitakāma), the Conqueror of the Senses (Jitendriya). (86)
contentBlocks.meanings: हिरण्यबाहुश्च (hiraṇyabāhuśca) – golden-armed and (हिरण्य:golden | बाहुः:arm | च:and); तथा (tathā) – thus; गुहापालः (guhāpālaḥ) – protector of caves (गुहा:cave | पालः:protector); प्रवेशिनाम् (praveśinām) – of the entrants; प्रकृष्टारिर्महाहर्षो (prakṛṣṭārirmahāharṣo) – great joy, eminent enemy (प्रकृष्ट:eminent | अरिः:enemy | महाहर्षः:great joy); जितकामो (jitakāmo) – conqueror of desires (जित:conquered | कामः:desire); जितेन्द्रियः (jitendriyaḥ) – conqueror of senses (जित:conquered | इन्द्रियः:senses);

contentBlocks.original:
गान्धारश्च सुवासश्च तपःसक्तो रतिर्नरः ।
महागीतो महानृत्यो ह्यप्सरोगणसेवितः ॥ ८७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gāndhāraśca suvāsaśca tapaḥsaktō ratirnaraḥ |
mahāgītō mahānr̥tyō hyapsarōgaṇasēvitaḥ || 87 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Gāndhari (fragrant), the Sweet-smelling (Suvāsa), the Austerity-devoted (Tapaḥsakta), Pleasure (Rati), the Human (Nara), the Greatly Sung (Mahāgīta), the Greatly Dancing (Mahānṛtya), the Served by Hosts of Apsaras (Hyapsarogaṇasevita). (87)
contentBlocks.meanings: गान्धारश्च (gāndhāraśca) – Gandhara and; सुवासश्च (suvāsaśca) – well-dressed and; तपःसक्तो (tapaḥsakto) – devoted to penance (तपः:penance | सक्तो:devoted); रतिर्नरः (ratirnaraḥ) – man of pleasure (रतिः:pleasure | नरः:man); महागीतो (mahāgīto) – great song; महानृत्यो (mahānṛtyo) – great dance; ह्यप्सरोगणसेवितः (hyapsarogaṇasevitaḥ) – attended by groups of Apsaras (ह्य:indeed | अप्सरोगण:groups of Apsaras | सेवितः:attended);

contentBlocks.original:
महाकेतुर्महाधातुर्नैकसानुचरश्चलः ।
आवेदनीय आदेशः सर्वगन्धसुखावहः ॥ ८८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahākēturmahādhāturnaikasānucaraścalaḥ |
āvēdanīya ādēśaḥ sarvagandhasukhāvahaḥ || 88 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Great Banner (Mahāketu), the Great Element (Mahādhātu), the Wanderer on Many Peaks (Naikasānucara), the Moving (Cala), the To-be-proclaimed (Āvedanīya), the Command (Ādeśa), the Bearer of All Fragrances and Pleasures (Sarvagandhasukhāvaha). (88)
contentBlocks.meanings: महाकेतुर्महाधातुर्नैकसानुचरश्चलः (mahāketurmahādhāturnaikasānucaraścalaḥ) – great comet, great element, many peaks, moving (महाकेतुः:great comet | महाधातुः:great element | नैकसानुचरः:many peaks | चलः:moving); आवेदनीय (āvedanīya) – to be informed; आदेशः (ādeśaḥ) – command; सर्वगन्धसुखावहः (sarvagandhasukhāvahaḥ) – bringer of all fragrant joy (सर्व:all | गन्ध:fragrance | सुख:joy | आवहः:bringer);

contentBlocks.original:
तोरणस्तारणो वातः परिधी पतिखेचरः ।
सम्योगो वर्धनो वृद्धो अतिवृद्धो गुणाधिकः ॥ ८९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tōraṇastāraṇō vātaḥ paridhī patikhēcaraḥ |
samyōgō vardhanō vr̥ddhō ativr̥ddhō guṇādhikaḥ || 89 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Gateway (Toraṇa), the Liberator (Tāraṇa), the Wind (Vāta), the Circumference (Paridhi), the Travelling Lord (Patikhecara), the Conjunction (Samyoga), the Increaser (Vardhana), the Grown (Vṛddha), the Greatly Grown (Ativṛddha), the Virtue-excelling (Guṇādhika). (89)
contentBlocks.meanings: तोरणस्तारणो (toraṇastāraṇo) – gate and savior (तोरण:gate | तारण:savior); वातः (vātaḥ) – wind; परिधी (paridhī) – enclosures; पतिखेचरः (patikhecaraḥ) – lord of sky wanderers (पति:lord | खेचर:sky wanderer); सम्योगो (samyogo) – union; वर्धनो (vardhano) – enhancer; वृद्धो (vṛddho) – grown; अतिवृद्धो (ativṛddho) – greatly grown (अति:greatly | वृद्ध:grown); गुणाधिकः (guṇādhikaḥ) – superior in qualities (गुण:qualities | अधिक:superior);

contentBlocks.original:
नित्य आत्मसहायश्च देवासुरपतिः पतिः ।
युक्तश्च युक्तबाहुश्च देवो दिविसुपर्वणः ॥ ९० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nitya ātmasahāyaśca dēvāsurapatiḥ patiḥ |
yuktaśca yuktabāhuśca dēvō divisuparvaṇaḥ || 90 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Eternal (Nitya), the Self-helped (Ātmasahāya), the Lord of Gods and Demons (Devāsurapati), the Lord (Pati), the Conjoined (Yukta), the Conjoined-armed (Yuktabāhu), the God of the Heavenly Auspicious Occasions (Devo Divisuparvaṇa). (90)
contentBlocks.meanings: नित्य (nitya) – eternal; आत्मसहायश्च (ātmasahāyaśca) – self-supporting (आत्म:self | सहाय:supporting | च:and); देवासुरपतिः (devāsurapatiḥ) – lord of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | पतिः:lord); पतिः (patiḥ) – lord; युक्तश्च (yuktaśca) – united and (युक्त:united | च:and); युक्तबाहुश्च (yuktabāhuśca) – strong-armed and (युक्त:united | बाहु:arm | च:and); देवो (devo) – god; दिविसुपर्वणः (divisuparvaṇaḥ) – lord of celestial paths (दिवि:celestial | सुपर्वणः:lord of paths);

contentBlocks.original:
आषाढश्च सुषाढश्च ध्रुवोऽथ हरिणो हरः ।
वपुरावर्तमानेभ्यो वसुश्रेष्ठो महापथः ॥ ९१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āṣāḍhaśca suṣāḍhaśca dhruvō:’tha hariṇō haraḥ |
vapurāvartamānēbhyō vasuśrēṣṭhō mahāpathaḥ || 91 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Āṣāḍha (the steadfast), Suṣāḍha (the very steadfast), the Immovable (Dhruva), the Deer (Hariṇa), Hara, the Body turned round and round (Vapurāvartamānebhya), the Best of the Vasus (Vasuśreṣṭha), the Great Path (Mahāpatha). (91)
contentBlocks.meanings: आषाढश्च (āṣāḍhaśca) – Āṣāḍha and; सुषाढश्च (suṣāḍhaśca) – Suṣāḍha and; ध्रुवोऽथ (dhruvo'tha) – Dhruva then; हरिणो (hariṇo) – deer; हरः (haraḥ) – Shiva; वपुरावर्तमानेभ्यो (vapurāvartamānebhyo) – from the revolving forms (वपुः:form | आवर्तमानेभ्यः:revolving); वसुश्रेष्ठो (vasuśreṣṭho) – best of wealth; महापथः (mahāpathaḥ) – great path;

contentBlocks.original:
शिरोहारी विमर्शश्च सर्वलक्षणलक्षितः ।
अक्षश्च रथयोगी च सर्वयोगी महाबलः ॥ ९२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śirōhārī vimarśaśca sarvalakṣaṇalakṣitaḥ |
akṣaśca rathayōgī ca sarvayōgī mahābalaḥ || 92 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Head-adorned (Śirohārī), the Deliberative (Vimarśa), the Marked with All Marks (Sarvalakṣaṇalakṣita), the Axle (Akṣa), the Chariot-yoked (Rathayogī), the All-yoked (Sarvayogī), the Greatly Powerful (Mahābala). (92)
contentBlocks.meanings: शिरोहारी (śirohārī) – head conqueror; विमर्शश्च (vimarśaśca) – and reflection; सर्वलक्षणलक्षितः (sarvalakṣaṇalakṣitaḥ) – marked by all signs (सर्व:all | लक्षण:signs | लक्षितः:marked); अक्षश्च (akṣaśca) – and axle; रथयोगी (rathayogī) – chariot practitioner (रथ:chariot | योगी:practitioner); च (ca) – and; सर्वयोगी (sarvayogī) – universal practitioner (सर्व:all | योगी:practitioner); महाबलः (mahābalaḥ) – great strength;

contentBlocks.original:
समाम्नायोऽसमाम्नायस्तीर्थदेवो महारथः ।
निर्जीवो जीवनो मन्त्रः शुभाक्षो बहुकर्कशः ॥ ९३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samāmnāyō:’samāmnāyastīrthadēvō mahārathaḥ |
nirjīvō jīvanō mantraḥ śubhākṣō bahukarkaśaḥ || 93 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Tradition (Samāmnāya), the Beyond-tradition (Asamāmnāya), the Sacred-ford God (Tīrthadeva), the Great Chariot (Mahāratha), the Non-living (Nirjīva), the Living (Jīvana), the Mantra, the Auspicious-eyed (Śubhākṣa), the Very Harsh (Bahukarkaśa). (93)
contentBlocks.meanings: समाम्नायोऽसमाम्नायस्तीर्थदेवो (samāmnāyo'samāmnāyastīrthadevo) – god of sacred places (समाम्नाय:Veda | असमाम्नाय:non-Vedic | तीर्थदेव:god of sacred places); महारथः (mahārathaḥ) – great charioteer; निर्जीवो (nirjīvo) – lifeless; जीवनो (jīvano) – life-giving; मन्त्रः (mantraḥ) – sacred chant; शुभाक्षो (śubhākṣo) – auspicious-eyed; बहुकर्कशः (bahukarkaśaḥ) – very harsh;

contentBlocks.original:
रत्नप्रभूतो रक्ताङ्गो महार्णवनिपानवित् । [रत्नाङ्गो]
मूलं विशालो ह्यमृतो व्यक्ताव्यक्तस्तपोनिधिः ॥ ९४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ratnaprabhūtō raktāṅgō mahārṇavanipānavit | [ratnāṅgō]
mūlaṁ viśālō hyamr̥tō vyaktāvyaktastapōnidhiḥ || 94 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Abundantly Jewelled (Ratnaprabhūta), the Red-limbed (Raktāṅga), the Knower of Bathing in the Great Ocean (Mahārṇavanipānavit), the Root (Mūla), the Vast (Viśāla), the Immortal (Amṛta), the Manifest and Unmanifest (Vyaktāvyakta), the Treasury of Austerity (Tapōnidhi). (94)
contentBlocks.meanings: रत्नप्रभूतो (ratnaprabhūto) – abundant in jewels (रत्न:jewel | प्रभूत:abundant); रक्ताङ्गो (raktāṅgo) – red-bodied (रक्त:red | अङ्ग:body); महार्णवनिपानवित् (mahārṇavanipānavit) – knower of ocean drinking (महा:great | अर्णव:ocean | निपान:drinking | वित्:knower); रत्नाङ्गो (ratnāṅgo) – jewel-bodied (रत्न:jewel | अङ्ग:body); मूलं (mūlaṁ) – root; विशालो (viśālo) – vast; ह्यमृतो (hyamṛto) – immortal; व्यक्ताव्यक्तस्तपोनिधिः (vyaktāvyaktastaponidhiḥ) – manifest-unmanifest penance treasure (व्यक्त:manifest | अव्यक्त:unmanifest | तपोनिधि:treasure of penance);

contentBlocks.original:
आरोहणोऽधिरोहश्च शीलधारी महायशाः ।
सेनाकल्पो महाकल्पो योगो युगकरो हरिः ॥ ९५ ॥ [योगकरो]
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ārōhaṇō:’dhirōhaśca śīladhārī mahāyaśāḥ |
sēnākalpō mahākalpō yōgō yugakarō hariḥ || 95 || [yōgakarō]
contentBlocks.translation: The Ascending (Ārohaṇa), the Overcoming (Adhiroha), the Character-bearer (Śīladhārī), the Greatly Renowned (Mahāyaśa), the Army-plan (Senākalpa), the Great Plan (Mahākalpa), Yoga, the Age-maker (Yugakara), Hari. (95)
contentBlocks.meanings: आरोहणोऽधिरोहश्च (ārohaṇo'dhirohaśca) – ascent and mounting (आरोहण:ascent | अधिरोह:mounting); शीलधारी (śīladhārī) – virtuous bearer (शील:virtue | धारी:bearer); महायशाः (mahāyaśāḥ) – great fame; सेनाकल्पो (senākalpo) – army-like (सेना:army | कल्प:like); महाकल्पो (mahākalpo) – great creation (मह:great | कल्प:creation); योगो (yogo) – union; युगकरो (yugakaro) – creator of ages (युग:age | कर:creator); हरिः (hariḥ) – Hari (Vishnu);

contentBlocks.original:
युगरूपो महारूपो महानागहनो वधः ।
न्यायनिर्वपणः पादः पण्डितो ह्यचलोपमः ॥ ९६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yugarūpō mahārūpō mahānāgahanō vadhaḥ |
nyāyanirvapaṇaḥ pādaḥ paṇḍitō hyacalōpamaḥ || 96 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Age-form (Yugarūpa), the Great Form (Mahārūpa), the Great Snake Slayer (Mahānāgahana), the Slaying (Vadha), the Just Distributor (Nyāyanirvapaṇa), the Foundation (Pāda), the Learned (Paṇḍita), the Mountain-like (Hyacalopama). (96)
contentBlocks.meanings: युगरूपो (yugarūpo) – form of an era (युग:era | रूप:form); महारूपो (mahārūpo) – great form (मह:great | रूप:form); महानागहनो (mahānāgaghano) – dense with great serpents (मह:great | नाग:serpent | घन:dense); वधः (vadhaḥ) – destruction; न्यायनिर्वपणः (nyāyanirvapaṇaḥ) – dispenser of justice (न्याय:justice | निर्वपण:dispenser); पादः (pādaḥ) – foot; पण्डितो (paṇḍito) – scholar; ह्यचलोपमः (hyacalopamaḥ) – like a mountain (अचल:mountain | उपम:like);

contentBlocks.original:
बहुमालो महामालः शशी हरसुलोचनः ।
विस्तारो लवणः कूपस्त्रियुगः सफलोदयः ॥ ९७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bahumālō mahāmālaḥ śaśī harasulōcanaḥ |
vistārō lavaṇaḥ kūpastriyugaḥ saphalōdayaḥ || 97 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Many-garlanded (Bahumāla), the Great Garland (Mahāmāla), the Moon (Śaśī), Hara's Bright Eye (Harasulōcana), the Expansive (Vistāra), the Salt (Lavaṇa), the Well (Kūpa), the Three-aged (Triyuga), the Fruitful (Saphalodaya). (97)
contentBlocks.meanings: बहुमालो (bahumālaḥ) – many garlands; महामालः (mahāmālaḥ) – great garland; शशी (śaśī) – moon; हरसुलोचनः (harasulocanaḥ) – eye of Shiva (हर:Shiva | सुलोचनः:beautiful-eyed); विस्तारो (vistāraḥ) – expansion; लवणः (lavaṇaḥ) – salt; कूपस्त्रियुगः (kūpastriyugaḥ) – well of three ages (कूप:well | त्रियुगः:three ages); सफलोदयः (saphalodayaḥ) – fruitful rise (सफल:fruitful | उदयः:rise);

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिलोचनो विषण्णाङ्गो मणिविद्धो जटाधरः ।
बिन्दुर्विसर्गः सुमुखः शरः सर्वायुधः सहः ॥ ९८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trilōcanō viṣaṇṇāṅgō maṇividdhō jaṭādharaḥ |
bindurvisargaḥ sumukhaḥ śaraḥ sarvāyudhaḥ sahaḥ || 98 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Three-eyed (Trilōcana), the Sorrow-limbed (Viṣaṇṇāṅga), the Jewel-pierced (Maṇividdha), the Matted-haired (Jaṭādhara), the Bindu, the Visarga, the Beautiful-faced (Sumukha), the Arrow (Śara), All Weapons (Sarvāyudha), Endurance (Saha). (98)
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रिलोचनो (trilocanaḥ) – three-eyed; विषण्णाङ्गो (viṣaṇṇāṅgaḥ) – with distressed limbs; मणिविद्धो (maṇividdhaḥ) – pierced by jewels; जटाधरः (jaṭādharaḥ) – wearing matted hair; बिन्दुर्विसर्गः (bindurvisargaḥ) – dot and visarga; सुमुखः (sumukhaḥ) – with a beautiful face; शरः (śaraḥ) – arrow; सर्वायुधः (sarvāyudhaḥ) – with all weapons (सर्व:all | आयुधः:weapons); सहः (sahaḥ) – enduring;

contentBlocks.original:
निवेदनः सुखाजातः सुगन्धारो महाधनुः ।
गन्धपाली च भगवानुत्थानः सर्वकर्मणाम् ॥ ९९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nivēdanaḥ sukhājātaḥ sugandhārō mahādhanuḥ |
gandhapālī ca bhagavānutthānaḥ sarvakarmaṇām || 99 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Offering (Nivedana), the Easily Born (Sukhājāta), the Beautiful Fragrant (Sugandhāra), the Great Bow (Mahādhanu), the Fragrance-guardian (Gandhapālī), the Blessed One (Bhagavān), the Arising of All Actions (Utthānaḥ Sarvakarmaṇām). (99)
contentBlocks.meanings: निवेदनः (nivedanaḥ) – offering; सुखाजातः (sukhajātaḥ) – born of happiness (सुख:happiness | जातः:born); सुगन्धारो (sugandhāraḥ) – fragrant; महाधनुः (mahādhanuḥ) – great bow (मह:great | धनुः:bow); गन्धपाली (gandhapālī) – fragrance protector; च (ca) – and; भगवानुत्थानः (bhagavānuttānaḥ) – resurrection of the Lord (भगवान्:Lord | उत्थानः:resurrection); सर्वकर्मणाम् (sarvakarmaṇām) – of all actions;

contentBlocks.original:
मन्थानो बहुलो वायुः सकलः सर्वलोचनः ।
तलस्तालः करस्थाली ऊर्ध्वसंहननो महान् ॥ १०० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
manthānō bahulō vāyuḥ sakalaḥ sarvalōcanaḥ |
talastālaḥ karasthālī ūrdhvasaṁhananō mahān || 100 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Churner (Manthāna), the Many (Bahula), Wind (Vāyu), the Complete (Sakala), the All-eyed (Sarvalōcana), the Ground (Tala), the Palm (Tāla), the Hand-plate (Karasthālī), the Upward-united (Ūrdhvasaṁhana), the Great (Mahān). (100)
contentBlocks.meanings: मन्थानो (manthānaḥ) – churner; बहुलो (bahulaḥ) – abundant; वायुः (vāyuḥ) – wind; सकलः (sakalaḥ) – entire; सर्वलोचनः (sarvalocanaḥ) – all-seeing; तलस्तालः (talastālaḥ) – palm tree; करस्थाली (karasthālī) – hand-held dish; ऊर्ध्वसंहननो (ūrdhvasaṃhananaḥ) – upright; महान् (mahān) – great;

contentBlocks.original:
छत्रं सुच्छत्रो विख्यातो लोकः सर्वाश्रयः क्रमः ।
मुण्डो विरूपो विकृतो दण्डी कुण्डी विकुर्वणः ॥ १०१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chatraṁ succhatrō vikhyātō lōkaḥ sarvāśrayaḥ kramaḥ |
muṇḍō virūpō vikr̥tō daṇḍī kuṇḍī vikurvaṇaḥ || 101 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Umbrella (Chatra), the Beautiful Umbrella (Succhattra), the Renowned (Vikhyāta), the World (Loka), the Universal Refuge (Sarvāśraya), Progression (Krama), the Shaven (Muṇḍa), the Deformed (Virūpa), the Distorted (Vikṛta), the Staff-bearer (Daṇḍī), the Pot-bearer (Kuṇḍī), the Transformer (Vikurvaṇa). (101)
contentBlocks.meanings: छत्रं (chatraṁ) – umbrella; सुच्छत्रो (succhatro) – good umbrella (सु:good | छत्रो:umbrella); विख्यातो (vikhyāto) – famous; लोकः (lokaḥ) – world; सर्वाश्रयः (sarvāśrayaḥ) – support of all (सर्व:all | आश्रयः:support); क्रमः (kramaḥ) – order; मुण्डो (muṇḍo) – bald; विरूपो (virūpo) – deformed; विकृतो (vikṛto) – distorted; दण्डी (daṇḍī) – staff-bearer; कुण्डी (kuṇḍī) – pot-bearer; विकुर्वणः (vikurvaṇaḥ) – transforming;

contentBlocks.original:
हर्यक्षः ककुभो वज्री शतजिह्वः सहस्रपात् ।
सहस्रमूर्धा देवेन्द्रः सर्वदेवमयो गुरुः ॥ १०२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
haryakṣaḥ kakubhō vajrī śatajihvaḥ sahasrapāt |
sahasramūrdhā dēvēndraḥ sarvadēvamayō guruḥ || 102 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Haryakṣa (the lion-eyed), the Pinnacled (Kakubha), the Thunderbolt-holder (Vajrī), the Hundred-tongued (Śatajihva), the Thousand-footed (Sahasrapāt), the Thousand-headed (Sahasramūrdha), the Lord of the Gods (Devendra), the God of All Gods (Sarvadēvamaya), the Teacher (Guru). (102)
contentBlocks.meanings: हर्यक्षः (haryakṣaḥ) – green-eyed; ककुभः (kakubhaḥ) – mountain; वज्री (vajrī) – wielder of the thunderbolt; शतजिह्वः (śatajihvaḥ) – hundred-tongued; सहस्रपात् (sahasrapāt) – thousand-footed; सहस्रमूर्धा (sahasramūrdhā) – thousand-headed; देवेन्द्रः (devendraḥ) – lord of the gods; सर्वदेवमयो (sarvadevamayaḥ) – embodiment of all gods (सर्व:all | देव:god | मय:consisting of); गुरुः (guruḥ) – teacher;

contentBlocks.original:
सहस्रबाहुः सर्वाङ्गः शरण्यः सर्वलोककृत् ।
पवित्रं त्रिककुन्मन्त्रः कनिष्ठः कृष्णपिङ्गलः ॥ १०३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sahasrabāhuḥ sarvāṅgaḥ śaraṇyaḥ sarvalōkakr̥t |
pavitraṁ trikakunmantraḥ kaniṣṭhaḥ kr̥ṣṇapiṅgalaḥ || 103 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Thousand-armed (Sahasrabāhu), the All-limbed (Sarvāṅga), the Refuge (Śaraṇya), the Maker of All Worlds (Sarvalōkakṛt), the Pure (Pavitra), the Mantra of the Three Peaks (Trikakunmantra), the Smallest (Kaniṣṭha), the Dark-tawny (Kṛṣṇapiṅgala). (103)
contentBlocks.meanings: सहस्रबाहुः (sahasrabāhuḥ) – thousand-armed (सहस्र:thousand | बाहुः:armed); सर्वाङ्गः (sarvāṅgaḥ) – all-limbed (सर्व:all | अङ्गः:limbed); शरण्यः (śaraṇyaḥ) – refuge; सर्वलोककृत् (sarvalokakṛt) – creator of all worlds (सर्व:all | लोक:worlds | कृत्:creator); पवित्रं (pavitraṁ) – pure; त्रिककुन्मन्त्रः (trikakunmantraḥ) – mantra of three peaks (त्रिक:three | कुन:peaks | मन्त्रः:mantra); कनिष्ठः (kaniṣṭhaḥ) – youngest; कृष्णपिङ्गलः (kṛṣṇapiṅgalaḥ) – dark and tawny (कृष्ण:dark | पिङ्गलः:tawny);

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मदण्डविनिर्माता शतघ्नीपाशशक्तिमान् ।
पद्मगर्भो महागर्भो ब्रह्मगर्भो जलोद्भवः ॥ १०४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmadaṇḍavinirmātā śataghnīpāśaśaktimān |
padmagarbhō mahāgarbhō brahmagarbhō jalōdbhavaḥ || 104 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Maker of Brahmā's Staff (Brahmadaṇḍavinirmātā), the Bearer of Śataghnī, Noose and Spear (Śataghṇīpāśaśaktimān), the Lotus-wombed (Padmagarbha), the Great-wombed (Mahāgarbha), the Brahma-wombed (Brahmāgarbha), the Water-born (Jalōdbhava). (104)
contentBlocks.meanings: ब्रह्मदण्डविनिर्माता (brahmadaṇḍavinirmātā) – creator of Brahma's staff (ब्रह्म:Brahma | दण्ड:staff | विनिर्माता:creator); शतघ्नीपाशशक्तिमान् (śataghnīpāśaśaktimān) – powerful with noose and weapon (शतघ्नी:weapon | पाश:noose | शक्तिमान्:powerful); पद्मगर्भो (padmagarbho) – lotus womb; महागर्भो (mahāgarbho) – great womb; ब्रह्मगर्भो (brahmagarbho) – Brahma's womb; जलोद्भवः (jalodbhavaḥ) – born of water;

contentBlocks.original:
गभस्तिर्ब्रह्मकृद्ब्रह्मी ब्रह्मविद्ब्राह्मणो गतिः ।
अनन्तरूपो नैकात्मा तिग्मतेजाः स्वयम्भुवः ॥ १०५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gabhastirbrahmakr̥dbrahmī brahmavidbrāhmaṇō gatiḥ |
anantarūpō naikātmā tigmatējāḥ svayambhuvaḥ || 105 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Ray (Gabhasti), the Brahma-maker (Brahmakṛt), the Brahmī (relating to Brahma), the Knower of Brahman (Brahmavid), the Brāhmaṇa, the Goal (Gati), the Infinite-form (Anantarūpa), the Not-one-self (Naikātmā), the Sharp-brilliant (Tigmatejā), the Self-born (Svayambhuva). (105)
contentBlocks.meanings: गभस्तिर्ब्रह्मकृद्ब्रह्मी (gabhastirbrahmakṛdbrahmī) – creator of Brahma (गभस्तिः:ray | ब्रह्मकृत्:creator of Brahma | ब्रह्मी:related to Brahma); ब्रह्मविद्ब्राह्मणो (brahmavidbrāhmaṇo) – knower of Brahman (ब्रह्मविद्:knower of Brahman | ब्राह्मणः:Brahmin); गतिः (gatiḥ) – goal; अनन्तरूपो (anantarūpo) – of infinite forms (अनन्त:infinite | रूपः:form); नैकात्मा (naikātmā) – many-souled (न:not | एक:one | आत्मा:soul); तिग्मतेजाः (tigmatejāḥ) – fierce brilliance (तिग्म:fierce | तेजाः:brilliance); स्वयम्भुवः (svayambhuvaḥ) – self-born;

contentBlocks.original:
ऊर्ध्वगात्मा पशुपतिर्वातरंहा मनोजवः ।
चन्दनी पद्मनालाग्रः सुरभ्युत्तरणो नरः ॥ १०६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ūrdhvagātmā paśupatirvātaraṁhā manōjavaḥ |
candanī padmanālāgraḥ surabhyuttaraṇō naraḥ || 106 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Upward-going Self (Ūrdhvagātmā), the Lord of Creatures (Paśupati), the Wind-runner (Vātaraṁhā), the Mind-swift (Manojavā), the Sandalwood-anointed (Candanī), the Lotus-stalked (Padmanālāgra), the Liberator across Fragrance (Surabhyuttaraṇa), the Human (Nara). (106)
contentBlocks.meanings: ऊर्ध्वगात्मा (ūrdhvagātmā) – upward-moving soul (ऊर्ध्व:upward | आत्मा:soul); पशुपतिर्वातरंहा (paśupatirvātaraṃhā) – lord of beings, wind-speed (पशुपति:lord of beings | वातरंहा:wind-speed); मनोजवः (manojavaḥ) – speed of mind; चन्दनी (candanī) – sandalwood; पद्मनालाग्रः (padmanālāgraḥ) – tip of lotus stalk (पद्म:lotus | नाल:stalk | अग्रः:tip); सुरभ्युत्तरणो (surabhyuttaraṇo) – fragrant ascent (सुरभि:fragrant | उत्तरण:ascent); नरः (naraḥ) – man;

contentBlocks.original:
कर्णिकारमहास्रग्वी नीलमौलिः पिनाकधृत् ।
उमापतिरुमाकान्तो जाह्नवीधृदुमाधवः ॥ १०७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karṇikāramahāsragvī nīlamauliḥ pinākadhr̥t |
umāpatirumākāntō jāhnavīdhr̥dumādhavaḥ || 107 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Karṇikāra-great-garlanded (Karṇikāramahāsragvī), the Blue-crowned (Nīlamauliḥ), the Pinaka-holder (Pinākadhr̥t), the Lord of Umā (Umāpati), the Beloved of Umā (Umākānta), the Holder of the Jāhnavī (Jāhnavīdhṛt), Umādhava (the spring of Umā). (107)
contentBlocks.meanings: कर्णिकारमहास्रग्वी (karṇikāramahāsragvī) – wearer of a great garland (कर्णिकार:karṇikāra flower | महास्रग्:great garland | वी:wearer); नीलमौलिः (nīlamauliḥ) – blue-crowned; पिनाकधृत् (pinākadhṛt) – holder of Pinaka (पिनाक:Pinaka (Shiva's bow) | धृत्:holder); उमापतिरुमाकान्तो (umāpatirumākānto) – Uma's husband and beloved (उमापति:Uma's husband | उमाकान्त:Uma's beloved); जाह्नवीधृदुमाधवः (jāhnavīdhrudumādhavaḥ) – holder of Ganga, Madhava (जाह्नवी:Ganga | धृत्:holder | उमाधव:Madhava);

contentBlocks.original:
वरो वराहो वरदो वरेण्यः सुमहास्वनः ।
महाप्रसादो दमनः शत्रुहा श्वेतपिङ्गलः ॥ १०८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
varō varāhō varadō varēṇyaḥ sumahāsvanaḥ |
mahāprasādō damanaḥ śatruhā śvētapiṅgalaḥ || 108 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Boon (Vara), the Boar (Varāha), the Boon-giver (Varada), the Choicest (Vareṇya), the Great-voiced (Sumahāsvana), the Great Grace (Mahāprasāda), the Tamer (Damana), the Enemy-slayer (Śatruhā), the White-tawny (Śvetapiṅgala). (108)
contentBlocks.meanings: वरो (varo) – boon; वराहो (varāhaḥ) – boar; वरदो (varadaḥ) – grantor of boons; वरेण्यः (vareṇyaḥ) – most excellent; सुमहास्वनः (sumahāsvanaḥ) – having a great sound (सु:good | महास्वनः:great sound); महाप्रसादो (mahāprasādaḥ) – great grace (महाप्रसाद:great grace); दमनः (damanaḥ) – subduer; शत्रुहा (śatruhā) – destroyer of enemies (शत्रु:enemy | हा:destroyer); श्वेतपिङ्गलः (śvetapiṅgalaḥ) – white and tawny (श्वेत:white | पिङ्गल:tawny);

contentBlocks.original:
पीतात्मा परमात्मा च प्रयतात्मा प्रधानधृत् । [प्रीतात्मा]
सर्वपार्श्वमुखस्त्र्यक्षो धर्मसाधारणो वरः ॥ १०९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pītātmā paramātmā ca prayatātmā pradhānadhr̥t | [prītātmā]
sarvapārśvamukhastryakṣō dharmasādhāraṇō varaḥ || 109 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Yellow-self (Pītātmā), the Supreme Self (Paramātmā), the Endeavoring Self (Prayatātmā), the Bearer of the Primordial (Pradhānadhṛt), the All-sided-faced (Sarvapārśvamukha), the Three-eyed (Tryakṣa), the Common to Dharma (Dharmasādhāraṇa), the Boon (Vara). (109)
contentBlocks.meanings: पीतात्मा (pītātmā) – pure soul; परमात्मा (paramātmā) – supreme soul; च (ca) – and; प्रयतात्मा (prayatātmā) – self-controlled; प्रधानधृत् (pradhānadhṛt) – holder of the chief; प्रीतात्मा (prītātmā) – beloved soul; सर्वपार्श्वमुखस्त्र्यक्षो (sarvapārśvamukhastryakṣo) – facing all sides, three-eyed (सर्वपार्श्व:all sides | मुख:facing | त्र्यक्ष:three-eyed); धर्मसाधारणो (dharmasādhāraṇo) – common to all dharma (धर्म:dharma | साधारण:common); वरः (varaḥ) – excellent;

contentBlocks.original:
चराचरात्मा सूक्ष्मात्मा अमृतो गोवृषेश्वरः ।
साध्यर्षिर्वसुरादित्यो विवस्वान्सवितामृतः ॥ ११० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
carācarātmā sūkṣmātmā amr̥tō gōvr̥ṣēśvaraḥ |
sādhyarṣirvasurādityō vivasvānsavitāmr̥taḥ || 110 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Moving and Unmoving Self (Carācarātmā), the Subtle Self (Sūkṣmātmā), the Immortal (Amṛta), the Lord of the Sacred Bull (Govṛṣeśvara), the Sādhya (the perfectable), the Seer (Ṛṣi), Vasu, Āditya (the sun), Vivasvān (the shining one), Savitā (the vivifier), the Immortal (Amṛta). (110)
contentBlocks.meanings: चराचरात्मा (carācarātmā) – soul of moving and unmoving (चरा:moving | अचरा:unmoving | आत्मा:soul); सूक्ष्मात्मा (sūkṣmātmā) – subtle soul (सूक्ष्म:subtle | आत्मा:soul); अमृतो (amṛto) – immortal; गोवृषेश्वरः (govṛṣeśvaraḥ) – lord of cows and bulls (गो:cow | वृष:bull | ईश्वरः:lord); साध्यर्षिर्वसुरादित्यो (sādhyaṛṣirvasurādityo) – sage of Sādhyas, Vasu, Āditya (साध्य:Sādhyas | ऋषि:sage | वसु:Vasu | आदित्य:Āditya); विवस्वान्सवितामृतः (vivasvānsavitāmṛtaḥ) – Vivasvān, Savitā, immortal (विवस्वान्:Vivasvān | सविता:Savitā | अमृतः:immortal);

contentBlocks.original:
व्यासः सर्गः सुसङ्क्षेपो विस्तरः पर्ययो नरः ।
ऋतुः संवत्सरो मासः पक्षः सङ्ख्यासमापनः ॥ १११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vyāsaḥ sargaḥ susaṅkṣēpō vistaraḥ paryayō naraḥ |
r̥tuḥ saṁvatsarō māsaḥ pakṣaḥ saṅkhyāsamāpanaḥ || 111 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Vyāsa (the arranger), Creation (Sarga), the Brief Summary (Susaṁkṣepa), the Expansion (Vistara), the Revolution (Paryaya), the Human (Nara), the Season (Ṛtu), the Year (Saṁvatsara), the Month (Māsa), the Fortnight (Pakṣa), the Reckoner (Saṁkhyāsamāpana). (111)
contentBlocks.meanings: व्यासः (vyāsaḥ) – Vyasa; सर्गः (sargaḥ) – creation; सुसङ्क्षेपो (susaṅkṣepo) – concise summary (सु:good | सङ्क्षेपः:summary); विस्तरः (vistaraḥ) – expansion; पर्ययो (paryayo) – synonym; नरः (naraḥ) – man; ऋतुः (ṛtuḥ) – season; संवत्सरो (saṁvatsaro) – year; मासः (māsaḥ) – month; पक्षः (pakṣaḥ) – fortnight; सङ्ख्यासमापनः (saṅkhyāsamāpanaḥ) – completion of counting (सङ्ख्या:counting | समापनः:completion);

contentBlocks.original:
कला काष्ठा लवा मात्रा मुहूर्ताहः क्षपाः क्षणाः ।
विश्वक्षेत्रं प्रजाबीजं लिङ्गमाद्यस्तु निर्गमः ॥ ११२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalā kāṣṭhā lavā mātrā muhūrtāhaḥ kṣapāḥ kṣaṇāḥ |
viśvakṣētraṁ prajābījaṁ liṅgamādyastu nirgamaḥ || 112 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Digit (Kalā), the Instant (Kāṣṭhā), the Fraction (Lavā), the Measure (Mātra), the Moment (Muhūrta), the Day (Aha), the Night (Kṣapā), the Instant (Kṣaṇa), the Universal Field (Viśvakṣetra), the Seed of Creatures (Prajābīja), the Liṅga (the primordial sign), the Primordial, the Emanation (Nirgama). (112)
contentBlocks.meanings: कला (kalā) – part; काष्ठा (kāṣṭhā) – moment; लवा (lavā) – instant; मात्रा (mātrā) – measure; मुहूर्ताहः (muhūrtāḥ) – hours; क्षपाः (kṣapāḥ) – nights; क्षणाः (kṣaṇāḥ) – moments; विश्वक्षेत्रं (viśvakṣetraṁ) – universal field (विश्व:universal | क्षेत्रं:field); प्रजाबीजं (prajābījaṁ) – seed of beings (प्रजा:beings | बीजं:seed); लिङ्गमाद्यस्तु (liṅgamādyastu) – primordial sign (लिङ्गम्:sign | आद्य:primordial | तु:but); निर्गमः (nirgamaḥ) – exit;

contentBlocks.original:
सदसद्व्यक्तमव्यक्तं पिता माता पितामहः ।
स्वर्गद्वारं प्रजाद्वारं मोक्षद्वारं त्रिविष्टपम् ॥ ११३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadasadvyaktamavyaktaṁ pitā mātā pitāmahaḥ |
svargadvāraṁ prajādvāraṁ mōkṣadvāraṁ triviṣṭapam || 113 ||
contentBlocks.translation: the Existent and Non-existent (Sadasad), the Manifest and Unmanifest (Vyaktamavyakta), Father, Mother, Grandfather, the Gate to Heaven (Svargadvāra), the Gate to Progeny (Prajādvāra), the Gate to Liberation (Mokṣadvāra), the Triple Heaven (Triviṣṭapa). (113)
contentBlocks.meanings: सदसद्व्यक्तमव्यक्तं (sadasadvyaktamavyaktaṁ) – manifest and unmanifest (सत्:existence | असत्:non-existence | व्यक्तम्:manifest | अव्यक्तं:unmanifest); पिता (pitā) – father; माता (mātā) – mother; पितामहः (pitāmahaḥ) – grandfather; स्वर्गद्वारं (svargadvāraṁ) – gateway to heaven (स्वर्ग:heaven | द्वारं:gateway); प्रजाद्वारं (prajādvāraṁ) – gateway to progeny (प्रजा:progeny | द्वारं:gateway); मोक्षद्वारं (mokṣadvāraṁ) – gateway to liberation (मोक्ष:liberation | द्वारं:gateway); त्रिविष्टपम् (triviṣṭapam) – heaven;

contentBlocks.original:
निर्वाणं ह्लादनश्चैव ब्रह्मलोकः परा गतिः ।
देवासुरविनिर्माता देवासुरपरायणः ॥ ११४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nirvāṇaṁ hlādanaścaiva brahmalōkaḥ parā gatiḥ |
dēvāsuravinirmātā dēvāsuraparāyaṇaḥ || 114 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Liberation (Nirvāṇa), Delight (Hlādana), the World of Brahma (Brahmalōka), the Supreme Goal (Parā Gati), the Maker of Gods and Demons (Devāsuravinirmātā), the Goal of Gods and Demons (Devāsuraparāyaṇa). (114)
contentBlocks.meanings: निर्वाणं (nirvāṇaṁ) – liberation; ह्लादनश्चैव (hlādanaścaiva) – and also delight (ह्लादन:delight | च:and | एव:indeed); ब्रह्मलोकः (brahmalokaḥ) – realm of Brahma (ब्रह्म:Brahma | लोकः:realm); परा (parā) – supreme; गतिः (gatiḥ) – goal; देवासुरविनिर्माता (devāsuravinirmātā) – creator of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | विनिर्माता:creator); देवासुरपरायणः (devāsuraparāyaṇaḥ) – refuge of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | परायणः:refuge);

contentBlocks.original:
देवासुरगुरुर्देवो देवासुरनमस्कृतः ।
देवासुरमहामात्रो देवासुरगणाश्रयः ॥ ११५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvāsuragururdēvō dēvāsuranamaskr̥taḥ |
dēvāsuramahāmātrō dēvāsuragaṇāśrayaḥ || 115 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Guru of Gods and Demons (Devāsuraguru), the God of Gods and Demons (Deva), the Saluted by Gods and Demons (Devāsuranamasḳta), the Great Lord of Gods and Demons (Devāsuramahāmātra), the Refuge of Hosts of Gods and Demons (Devāsuragaṇāśraya). (115)
contentBlocks.meanings: देवासुरगुरुर्देवो (devāsuragururdevo) – god, teacher of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | गुरु:teacher | देव:god); देवासुरनमस्कृतः (devāsuranamaskṛtaḥ) – worshipped by gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | नमस्कृत:worshipped); देवासुरमहामात्रो (devāsuramahāmātro) – great minister of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | महामात्र:great minister); देवासुरगणाश्रयः (devāsuragaṇāśrayaḥ) – refuge of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | गण:group | आश्रय:refuge);

contentBlocks.original:
देवासुरगणाध्यक्षो देवासुरगणाग्रणीः ।
देवातिदेवो देवर्षिर्देवासुरवरप्रदः ॥ ११६ ॥ [देवादि]
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvāsuragaṇādhyakṣō dēvāsuragaṇāgraṇīḥ |
dēvātidēvō dēvarṣirdēvāsuravarapradaḥ || 116 || [dēvādi]
contentBlocks.translation: The Overseer of Hosts of Gods and Demons (Devāsuragaṇādhyakṣa), the Leader of Hosts of Gods and Demons (Devāsuragaṇāgraṇī), the God above Gods (Devātideva), the Divine Seer (Devarṣi), the Boon-giver to Gods and Demons (Devāsuravaraprada). (116)
contentBlocks.meanings: देवासुरगणाध्यक्षो (devāsuragaṇādhyakṣaḥ) – leader of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | गण:group | अध्यक्ष:leader); देवासुरगणाग्रणीः (devāsuragaṇāgraṇīḥ) – foremost among gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | गण:group | अग्रणी:foremost); देवातिदेवो (devātidevo) – supreme god (देव:god | अति:beyond | देव:god); देवर्षिर्देवासुरवरप्रदः (devarṣirdevāsuravarapradaḥ) – sage among gods, giver of boons to gods and demons (देवर्षि:sage among gods | देव:god | असुर:demon | वर:boon | प्रद:giver);

contentBlocks.original:
देवासुरेश्वरो विश्वो देवासुरमहेश्वरः ।
सर्वदेवमयोऽचिन्त्यो देवतात्माऽऽत्मसम्भवः ॥ ११७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvāsurēśvarō viśvō dēvāsuramahēśvaraḥ |
sarvadēvamayō:’cintyō dēvatātmā:’:’tmasambhavaḥ || 117 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Lord of Gods and Demons (Devāsureśvara), the Universe (Viśva), the Great Lord of Gods and Demons (Devāsuramāheśvara), the All-god-filled (Sarvadēvamaya), the Incomprehensible (Acintya), the God-self (Devatātmā), the Self-born (Ātmasambhava). (117)
contentBlocks.meanings: देवासुरेश्वरो (devāsureśvaro) – lord of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | ईश्वर:lord); विश्वो (viśvo) – universe; देवासुरमहेश्वरः (devāsuramaheśvaraḥ) – great lord of gods and demons (देव:god | असुर:demon | महेश्वर:great lord); सर्वदेवमयोऽचिन्त्यो (sarvadevamayo'cintyo) – embodiment of all gods, inconceivable (सर्व:all | देव:god | मय:embodiment | अचिन्त्य:inconceivable); देवतात्माऽऽत्मसम्भवः (devatātmā''tmasambhavaḥ) – divine soul, self-born (देवतात्मा:divine soul | आत्मसम्भव:self-born);

contentBlocks.original:
उद्भित्त्रिविक्रमो वैद्यो विरजो नीरजोऽमरः ।
ईड्यो हस्तीश्वरो व्याघ्रो देवसिंहो नरर्षभः ॥ ११८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
udbhittrivikramō vaidyō virajō nīrajō:’maraḥ |
īḍyō hastīśvarō vyāghrō dēvasiṁhō nararṣabhaḥ || 118 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Upward-burst (Udbhit), the Three-stepping (Trivikrama), the Physician (Vaidya), the Passionless (Viraja), the Lotus-born (Nīraja), the Immortal (Amara), the Praiseworthy (Īḍya), the Elephant-lord (Hastīśvara), the Tiger (Vyāghra), the God-lion (Devasiṁha), the Bull among Men (Nararṣabha). (118)
contentBlocks.meanings: उद्भित्त्रिविक्रमो (udbhittrivikramaḥ) – uplifter of three strides (उद्भित्:uplifter | त्रिविक्रम:three strides); वैद्यो (vaidyaḥ) – physician; विरजो (virajaḥ) – stainless; नीरजोऽमरः (nīrajo'maraḥ) – water-born immortal (नीरज:water-born | अमर:immortal); ईड्यो (īḍyaḥ) – praiseworthy; हस्तीश्वरो (hastīśvaraḥ) – lord of elephants (हस्ती:elephant | ईश्वर:lord); व्याघ्रो (vyāghraḥ) – tiger; देवसिंहो (devasimhaḥ) – lion among gods (देव:god | सिंह:lion); नरर्षभः (nararṣabhaḥ) – bull among men (नर:man | ऋषभ:bull);

contentBlocks.original:
विबुधोऽग्रवरः सूक्ष्मः सर्वदेवस्तपोमयः ।
सुयुक्तः शोभनो वज्री प्रासानां प्रभवोऽव्ययः ॥ ११९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vibudhō:’gravaraḥ sūkṣmaḥ sarvadēvastapōmayaḥ |
suyuktaḥ śōbhanō vajrī prāsānāṁ prabhavō:’vyayaḥ || 119 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The All-knowing (Vibudha), the Chief among the Best (Agravaraḥ), the Subtle (Sūkṣma), All Gods (Sarvadeva), the Austerity-filled (Tapomaya), the Well-yoked (Suyukta), the Auspicious (Śobhana), the Thunderbolt-holder (Vajrī), the Source of Spears (Prāsānāṁ Prabhava), the Imperishable (Avyaya). (119)
contentBlocks.meanings: विबुधोऽग्रवरः (vibudho'gravaraḥ) – foremost among the wise (विबुध:wise | अग्रवर:foremost); सूक्ष्मः (sūkṣmaḥ) – subtle; सर्वदेवस्तपोमयः (sarvadevastapomayaḥ) – essence of all gods' austerity (सर्वदेव:all gods | तपोमय:essence of austerity); सयुक्तः (suyuktaḥ) – well-composed; शोभनो (śobhano) – auspicious; वज्री (vajrī) – wielder of the thunderbolt; प्रासानां (prāsānāṁ) – of the spears; प्रभवोऽव्ययः (prabhavo'vyayaḥ) – imperishable origin (प्रभव:origin | अव्यय:imperishable);

contentBlocks.original:
गुहः कान्तो निजः सर्गः पवित्रं सर्वपावनः ।
शृङ्गी शृङ्गप्रियो बभ्रू राजराजो निरामयः ॥ १२० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
guhaḥ kāntō nijaḥ sargaḥ pavitraṁ sarvapāvanaḥ |
śr̥ṅgī śr̥ṅgapriyō babhrū rājarājō nirāmayaḥ || 120 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Guha (the secret one), the Beloved (Kānta), the Self-created (Nija), Creation (Sarga), the Pure (Pavitra), the Purifier of All (Sarvapāvana), the Horned (Śṛṅgī), the Horn-lover (Śṛṅgapriya), the Brown (Babhru), the King of Kings (Rājarāja), the Disease-free (Nirāmaya). (120)
contentBlocks.meanings: गुहः (guhaḥ) – cave-dweller; कान्तो (kāntaḥ) – beloved; निजः (nijaḥ) – own; सर्गः (sargaḥ) – creation; पवित्रं (pavitraṁ) – pure; सर्वपावनः (sarvapāvanaḥ) – purifier of all (सर्व:all | पावनः:purifier); शृङ्गी (śṛṅgī) – horned; शृङ्गप्रियो (śṛṅgapriyaḥ) – fond of peaks (शृङ्ग:peak | प्रियो:fond of); बभ्रू (babhrū) – brown; राजराजो (rājarājaḥ) – king of kings (राज:king | राजो:king); निरामयः (nirāmayaḥ) – free from disease;

contentBlocks.original:
अभिरामः सुरगणो विरामः सर्वसाधनः ।
ललाटाक्षो विश्वदेवो हरिणो ब्रह्मवर्चसः ॥ १२१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
abhirāmaḥ suragaṇō virāmaḥ sarvasādhanaḥ |
lalāṭākṣō viśvadēvō hariṇō brahmavarcasaḥ || 121 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Handsome (Abhirāma), the Host of Gods (Suragaṇa), the Cessation (Virāma), the All-means (Sarvasādhana), the Forehead-eyed (Lalāṭākṣa), the God of the Universe (Viśvadeva), the Deer (Hariṇa), the Brahmic Radiance (Brahmavarcasa). (121)
contentBlocks.meanings: अभिरामः (abhirāmaḥ) – charming; सुरगणो (suragaṇaḥ) – host of gods (सुर:god | गणः:host); विरामः (virāmaḥ) – cessation; सर्वसाधनः (sarvasādhanaḥ) – all-accomplishing (सर्व:all | साधनः:accomplishing); ललाटाक्षो (lalāṭākṣaḥ) – forehead-eyed (ललाट:forehead | अक्षः:eye); विश्वदेवो (viśvadevaḥ) – universal god (विश्व:universal | देवः:god); हरिणो (hariṇaḥ) – deer-like; ब्रह्मवर्चसः (brahmavarcasaḥ) – having Brahma's brilliance (ब्रह्म:Brahma | वर्चसः:brilliance);

contentBlocks.original:
स्थावराणां पतिश्चैव नियमेन्द्रियवर्धनः ।
सिद्धार्थः सिद्धभूतार्थोऽचिन्त्यः सत्यव्रतः शुचिः ॥ १२२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sthāvarāṇāṁ patiścaiva niyamēndriyavardhanaḥ |
siddhārthaḥ siddhabhūtārthō:’cintyaḥ satyavrataḥ śuciḥ || 122 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Lord of the Stationary (Sthāvarāṇāṁ Pati), the Augmenter of Disciplined Senses (Niyamendriyavardhana), the Fulfilled (Siddhārtha), the One Whose Purpose is the Perfected (Siddabhūtārtha), the Incomprehensible (Acintya), the Truth-vowed (Satyavrata), the Pure (Śuci). (122)
contentBlocks.meanings: स्थावराणां (sthāvarāṇāṁ) – of the immovable; पतिश्चैव (patiścaiva) – and indeed the lord (पतिः:lord | च:and | एव:indeed); नियमेन्द्रियवर्धनः (niyamendriyavardhanaḥ) – enhancer of controlled senses (नियम:control | इन्द्रिय:senses | वर्धनः:enhancer); सिद्धार्थः (siddhārthaḥ) – one whose goals are achieved; सिद्धभूतार्थोऽचिन्त्यः (siddhabhūtārtho'cintyaḥ) – unimaginable with achieved goals (सिद्ध:achieved | भूत:being | अर्थः:goal | अचिन्त्यः:unimaginable); सत्यव्रतः (satyavrataḥ) – truthful in vows; शुचिः (śuciḥ) – pure;

contentBlocks.original:
व्रताधिपः परं ब्रह्म भक्तानां परमा गतिः । [भक्तानुग्रहकारकः]
विमुक्तो मुक्ततेजाश्च श्रीमान् श्रीवर्धनो जगत् ॥ १२३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vratādhipaḥ paraṁ brahma bhaktānāṁ paramā gatiḥ | [bhaktānugrahakārakaḥ]
vimuktō muktatējāśca śrīmān śrīvardhanō jagat || 123 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Lord of Vows (Vratādhipa), the Supreme Brahman (Paraṁ Brahma), the Supreme Goal of Devotees (Bhaktānāṁ Paramā Gatiḥ), the Liberated (Vimukta), the Liberated Brilliance (Muktatejā), the Auspicious (Śrīmān), the Augmenter of Prosperity (Śrīvardhana), the World (Jagat). (123)
contentBlocks.meanings: व्रताधिपः (vratādhipaḥ) – lord of vows (व्रत:vow | अधिपः:lord); परं (paraṁ) – supreme; ब्रह्म (brahma) – Brahman; भक्तानां (bhaktānāṁ) – of devotees; परमा (paramā) – ultimate; गतिः (gatiḥ) – goal; भक्तानुग्रहकारकः (bhaktānugrahakārakaḥ) – bestower of grace to devotees (भक्त:devotee | अनुग्रह:grace | कारकः:bestower); विमुक्तो (vimukto) – liberated; मुक्ततेजाश्च (muktatejāśca) – and radiant with liberation (मुक्त:liberated | तेजाः:radiance | च:and); श्रीमान् (śrīmān) – prosperous; श्रीवर्धनो (śrīvardhano) – enhancer of prosperity (श्री:prosperity | वर्धन:enhancer); जगत् (jagat) – world;

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तरपीठिका (फलश्रुति)
यथा प्रधानं भगवानिति भक्त्या स्तुतो मया ।
यं न ब्रह्मादयो देवा विदुस्तत्त्वेन नर्षयः ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttarapīṭhikā (phalaśruti)
yathā pradhānaṁ bhagavāniti bhaktyā stutō mayā |
yaṁ na brahmādayō dēvā vidustattvēna narṣayaḥ || 1 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Phalaśruti: Thus have I praised with devotion the Blessed Lord as the Primordial. Yet even Brahmā and the gods, and the great seers, do not know his true nature in reality. (1)
contentBlocks.meanings: उत्तरपीठिका (uttarapīṭhikā) – epilogue (उत्तर:later | पीठिका:introduction); फलश्रुति (phalaśruti) – result of listening (फल:result | श्रुति:hearing); यथा (yathā) – as; प्रधानं (pradhānaṁ) – chief; भगवानिति (bhagavāniti) – as Bhagavan (भगवान्:Lord | इति:thus); भक्त्या (bhaktyā) – with devotion; स्तुतो (stuto) – praised; मया (mayā) – by me; यं (yaṁ) – whom; न (na) – not; ब्रह्मादयो (brahmādayo) – Brahma and others (ब्रह्मा:Brahma | आदयः:and others); देवा (devā) – gods; विदुस्तत्त्वेन (vidustattvena) – know truly (विदुः:know | तत्त्वेन:truly); नर्षयः (narṣayaḥ) – nor sages (न:not | ऋषयः:sages);

contentBlocks.original:
स्तोतव्यमर्च्यं वन्द्यं च कः स्तोष्यति जगत्पतिम् ।
भक्त्या त्वेवं पुरस्कृत्य मया यज्ञपतिर्विभुः ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stōtavyamarcyaṁ vandyaṁ ca kaḥ stōṣyati jagatpatim |
bhaktyā tvēvaṁ puraskr̥tya mayā yajñapatirvibhuḥ || 2 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Who is capable of praising, worshipping and saluting the Lord of the Universe — he who is worthy of praise, worship and reverence? Having thus with devotion propitiated the all-pervading Lord of sacrifice, (2)
contentBlocks.meanings: स्तोतव्यमर्च्यं (stotavyamarcyaṁ) – to be praised and worshipped (स्तोतव्यम्:to be praised | अर्च्यं:to be worshipped); वन्द्यं (vandyaṁ) – to be revered; च (ca) – and; कः (kaḥ) – who; स्तोष्यति (stoṣyati) – will praise; जगत्पतिम् (jagatpatim) – lord of the world (जगत्:world | पतिम्:lord); भक्त्या (bhaktyā) – with devotion; त्वेवं (tvevaṁ) – thus; पुरस्कृत्य (puraskṛtya) – placing in front; मया (mayā) – by me; यज्ञपतिर्विभुः (yajñapatirvibhuḥ) – lord of sacrifices, the omnipresent (यज्ञपतिः:lord of sacrifices | विभुः:omnipresent);

contentBlocks.original:
ततोऽभ्यनुज्ञां सम्प्राप्य स्तुतो मतिमतां वरः ।
शिवमेभिः स्तुवन् देवं नामभिः पुष्टिवर्धनैः ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tatō:’bhyanujñāṁ samprāpya stutō matimatāṁ varaḥ |
śivamēbhiḥ stuvan dēvaṁ nāmabhiḥ puṣṭivardhanaiḥ || 3 ||
contentBlocks.translation: and having received his gracious permission, the greatest of the wise praised the God Śiva with these names that bestow strength and growth — (3)
contentBlocks.meanings: ततोऽभ्यनुज्ञां (tato'bhyanujñāṁ) – then permission; सम्प्राप्य (samprāpya) – having obtained; स्तुतो (stuto) – praised; मतिमतां (matimatāṁ) – of the wise; वरः (varaḥ) – best; शिवमेभिः (śivamebhiḥ) – Shiva with these; स्तुवन् (stuvan) – praising; देवं (devaṁ) – god; नामभिः (nāmabhiḥ) – with names; पुष्टिवर्धनैः (puṣṭivardhanaiḥ) – nourishing;

contentBlocks.original:
नित्ययुक्तः शुचिर्भक्तः प्राप्नोत्यात्मानमात्मना ।
एतद्धि परमं ब्रह्म परं ब्रह्माधिगच्छति ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityayuktaḥ śucirbhaktaḥ prāpnōtyātmānamātmanā |
ētaddhi paramaṁ brahma paraṁ brahmādhigacchati || 4 ||
contentBlocks.translation: One who is constantly devoted, pure and faithful attains the Self through the self. This indeed is the Supreme Brahman; he reaches the Supreme Brahman. (4)
contentBlocks.meanings: नित्ययुक्तः (nityayuktaḥ) – eternally engaged; शुचिर्भक्तः (śucirbhaktaḥ) – pure devotee (शुचिः:pure | भक्तः:devotee); प्राप्नोत्यात्मानमात्मना (prāpnoty-ātmānam-ātmanā) – attains the self by the self (प्राप्नोति:attains | आत्मानम्:the self | आत्मना:by the self); एतद्धि (etaddhi) – this indeed; परमं (paramaṁ) – supreme; ब्रह्म (brahma) – Brahman; परं (paraṁ) – highest; ब्रह्माधिगच्छति (brahmādhigacchati) – attains Brahman (ब्रह्म:Brahman | अधिगच्छति:attains);

contentBlocks.original:
ऋषयश्चैव देवाश्च स्तुवन्त्येतेन तत्परम् ।
स्तूयमानो महादेवस्तुष्यते नियतात्मभिः ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
r̥ṣayaścaiva dēvāśca stuvantyētēna tatparam |
stūyamānō mahādēvastuṣyatē niyatātmabhiḥ || 5 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Both the seers and the gods praise that Supreme through this hymn. When praised thus, the Great God Mahādeva is pleased with those of disciplined minds. (5)
contentBlocks.meanings: ऋषयश्चैव (ṛṣayaścaiva) – and the sages (ऋषयः:sages | च:and | एव:indeed); देवाश्च (devāśca) – and the gods (देवाः:gods | च:and); स्तुवन्त्येतेन (stuvantyetena) – praise with this (स्तुवन्ति:praise | एतेन:with this); तत्परम् (tatparam) – that supreme; स्तूयमानो (stūyamāno) – being praised; महादेवस्तुष्यते (mahādevastuṣyate) – Mahadeva is pleased (महादेवः:Mahadeva | तुष्यते:is pleased); नियतात्मभिः (niyatātmabhiḥ) – by the self-controlled (नियत:controlled | आत्मभिः:by selves);

contentBlocks.original:
भक्तानुकम्पी भगवानात्मसंस्थाकरो विभुः ।
तथैव च मनुष्येषु ये मनुष्याः प्रधानतः ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaktānukampī bhagavānātmasaṁsthākarō vibhuḥ |
tathaiva ca manuṣyēṣu yē manuṣyāḥ pradhānataḥ || 6 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Blessed Lord, who is compassionate to his devotees and who establishes all in the Self, is all-pervading. And so too among human beings — those who are foremost among men, (6)
contentBlocks.meanings: भक्तानुकम्पी (bhaktānukampī) – compassionate to devotees (भक्त:devotee | अनुकम्पी:compassionate); भगवानात्मसंस्थाकरो (bhagavānātmasaṃsthākaro) – establisher in self (भगवान्:Lord | आत्म:self | संस्था:establishment | करो:maker); विभुः (vibhuḥ) – all-pervading; तथैव (tathaiva) – similarly; च (ca) – and; मनुष्येषु (manuṣyeṣu) – among men; ये (ye) – who; मनुष्याः (manuṣyāḥ) – men; प्रधानतः (pradhānataḥ) – chiefly;

contentBlocks.original:
आस्तिकाः श्रद्दधानाश्च बहुभिर्जन्मभिः स्तवैः ।
भक्त्या ह्यनन्यमीशानं परं देवं सनातनम् ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āstikāḥ śraddadhānāśca bahubhirjanmabhiḥ stavaiḥ |
bhaktyā hyananyamīśānaṁ paraṁ dēvaṁ sanātanam || 7 ||
contentBlocks.translation: those who are faithful believers, filled with faith, over many births and with many hymns, with unwavering devotion to the supreme eternal God Īśāna alone, (7)
contentBlocks.meanings: आस्तिकाः (āstikāḥ) – believers; श्रद्दधानाश्च (śraddadhānāśca) – and the faithful; बहुभिर्जन्मभिः (bahubhirjanmabhiḥ) – through many births (बहुभिः:many | जन्मभिः:births); स्तवैः (stavaiḥ) – praises; भक्त्या (bhaktyā) – with devotion; ह्यनन्यमीशानं (hyananyamīśānaṁ) – the supreme lord (ह्यनन्यम्:indeed exclusive | ईशानं:lord); परं (paraṁ) – supreme; देवं (devaṁ) – god; सनातनम् (sanātanam) – eternal;

contentBlocks.original:
कर्मणा मनसा वाचा भावेनामिततेजसः ।
शयाना जाग्रमाणाश्च व्रजन्नुपविशंस्तथा ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karmaṇā manasā vācā bhāvēnāmitatējasaḥ |
śayānā jāgramāṇāśca vrajannupaviśaṁstathā || 8 ||
contentBlocks.translation: through action, mind and speech, with feeling, toward the One of limitless brilliance — while lying down or waking, while walking or sitting, (8)
contentBlocks.meanings: कर्मणा (karmaṇā) – by action; मनसा (manasā) – by mind; वाचा (vācā) – by speech; भावेनामिततेजसः (bhāvenāmitatejasaḥ) – by the infinite splendor (भावेन:by feeling | अमिततेजसः:of infinite splendor); शयाना (śayānā) – lying down; जाग्रमाणाश्च (jāgramāṇāśca) – and being awake; व्रजन्नुपविशंस्तथा (vrajannupaviśaṁstathā) – going and sitting (व्रजन्:going | उपविशन्:sitting | तथा:and);

contentBlocks.original:
उन्मिषन्निमिषंश्चैव चिन्तयन्तः पुनः पुनः ।
शृण्वन्तः श्रावयन्तश्च कथयन्तश्च ते भवम् ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
unmiṣannimiṣaṁścaiva cintayantaḥ punaḥ punaḥ |
śr̥ṇvantaḥ śrāvayantaśca kathayantaśca tē bhavam || 9 ||
contentBlocks.translation: while opening and closing the eyes, again and again meditating, listening and causing others to listen, and narrating — they speak of Bhava. (9)
contentBlocks.meanings: उन्मिषन्निमिषंश्चैव (unmiṣannimiṣaṃścaiva) – opening and closing eyes (उन्मिषन्:opening | निमिषं:closing | च:and | एव:indeed); चिन्तयन्तः (cintayantaḥ) – contemplating; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; शृण्वन्तः (śṛṇvantaḥ) – hearing; श्रावयन्तश्च (śrāvayantaśca) – making others hear (श्रावयन्तः:making others hear | च:and); कथयन्तश्च (kathayantaśca) – telling (कथयन्तः:telling | च:and); ते (te) – they; भवम् (bhavam) – existence;

contentBlocks.original:
स्तुवन्तः स्तूयमानाश्च तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च ।
जन्मकोटिसहस्रेषु नानासंसारयोनिषु ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stuvantaḥ stūyamānāśca tuṣyanti ca ramanti ca |
janmakōṭisahasrēṣu nānāsaṁsārayōniṣu || 10 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Praising and being praised, they rejoice and delight. Over thousands of millions of births, in the various wombs of saṁsāra, (10)
contentBlocks.meanings: स्तुवन्तः (stuvantaḥ) – praising; स्तूयमानाश्च (stūyamānāśca) – being praised and (स्तूयमानाः:being praised | च:and); तुष्यन्ति (tuṣyanti) – are satisfied; च (ca) – and; रमन्ति (ramanti) – delight; च (ca) – and; जन्मकोटिसहस्रेषु (janmakoṭisahasreṣu) – in thousands of millions of births (जन्म:birth | कोटि:millions | सहस्रेषु:in thousands); नानासंसारयोनिषु (nānāsaṁsārayoniṣu) – in various worldly wombs (नाना:various | संसार:worldly | योनिषु:in wombs);

contentBlocks.original:
जन्तोर्विगतपापस्य भवे भक्तिः प्रजायते ।
उत्पन्ना च भवे भक्तिरनन्या सर्वभावतः ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jantōrvigatapāpasya bhavē bhaktiḥ prajāyatē |
utpannā ca bhavē bhaktirananyā sarvabhāvataḥ || 11 ||
contentBlocks.translation: when the creature's sins have departed, devotion to Bhava arises. And when devotion to Bhava arises, it is undivided and with the whole heart — (11)
contentBlocks.meanings: जन्तोर्विगतपापस्य (jantorvigatapāpasya) – of the sinless being (जन्तोः:of the being | विगत:devoid of | पापस्य:sin); भवे (bhave) – in existence; भक्तिः (bhaktiḥ) – devotion; प्रजायते (prajāyate) – arises; उत्पन्ना (utpannā) – arisen; च (ca) – and; भवे (bhave) – in existence; भक्तिरनन्या (bhaktirananyā) – undivided devotion (भक्तिः:devotion | अनन्या:undivided); सर्वभावतः (sarvabhāvataḥ) – with all feelings (सर्व:all | भावतः:feelings);

contentBlocks.original:
भाविनः कारणे चास्य सर्वयुक्तस्य सर्वथा ।
एतद्देवेषु दुष्प्रापं मनुष्येषु न लभ्यते ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhāvinaḥ kāraṇē cāsya sarvayuktasya sarvathā |
ētaddēvēṣu duṣprāpaṁ manuṣyēṣu na labhyatē || 12 ||
contentBlocks.translation: it becomes the cause for future good in all ways for one thus wholly united. This is difficult for the gods to attain, and not obtained among human beings — (12)
contentBlocks.meanings: भाविनः (bhāvinaḥ) – of the future; कारणे (kāraṇe) – in the cause; चास्य (cāsya) – and his; सर्वयुक्तस्य (sarvayuktasya) – of all-inclusive; सर्वथा (sarvathā) – in every way; एतद्देवेषु (etad deveṣu) – this among the gods (एतत्:this | देवेषु:among the gods); दुष्प्रापं (duṣprāpaṁ) – difficult to obtain; मनुष्येषु (manuṣyeṣu) – among humans; न (na) – not; लभ्यते (labhyate) – is obtained;

contentBlocks.original:
निर्विघ्ना निश्चला रुद्रे भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी ।
तस्यैव च प्रसादेन भक्तिरुत्पद्यते नृणाम् ॥ १३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nirvighnā niścalā rudrē bhaktiravyabhicāriṇī |
tasyaiva ca prasādēna bhaktirutpadyatē nr̥ṇām || 13 ||
contentBlocks.translation: that unwavering, undeviating devotion to Rudra, steady and constant. Only by his grace does devotion arise in human beings. (13)
contentBlocks.meanings: निर्विघ्ना (nirvighnā) – without obstacles; निश्चला (niścalā) – steadfast; रुद्रे (rudre) – in Rudra; भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी (bhaktiravyabhicāriṇī) – unwavering devotion (भक्ति:devotion | अव्यभिचारिणी:unwavering); तस्यैव (tasyaiva) – by his; च (ca) – and; प्रसादेन (prasādena) – by grace; भक्तिरुत्पद्यते (bhaktirutpadyate) – devotion arises (भक्ति:devotion | उत्पद्यते:arises); नृणाम् (nṛṇām) – of men;

contentBlocks.original:
येन यान्ति परां सिद्धिं तद्भावगतचेतसः ।
ये सर्वभावानुगताः प्रपद्यन्ते महेश्वरम् ॥ १४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yēna yānti parāṁ siddhiṁ tadbhāvagatacētasaḥ |
yē sarvabhāvānugatāḥ prapadyantē mahēśvaram || 14 ||
contentBlocks.translation: By that devotion those whose minds are absorbed in his nature reach the Supreme Perfection. Those who take refuge in Maheśvara with all their hearts (14)
contentBlocks.meanings: येन (yena) – by which; यान्ति (yānti) – attain; परां (parāṁ) – supreme; सिद्धिं (siddhiṁ) – perfection; तद्भावगतचेतसः (tadbhāvagatacētasaḥ) – minds absorbed in that state (तद्भाव:that state | गत:gone | चेतसः:minds); ये (ye) – who; सर्वभावानुगताः (sarvabhāvānugatāḥ) – following all states (सर्व:all | भाव:states | अनुगताः:following); प्रपद्यन्ते (prapadyante) – surrender; महेश्वरम् (maheśvaram) – to the great lord;

contentBlocks.original:
प्रपन्नवत्सलो देवः संसारात्तान्समुद्धरेत् ।
एवमन्ये विकुर्वन्ति देवाः संसारमोचनम् ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prapannavatsalō dēvaḥ saṁsārāttānsamuddharēt |
ēvamanyē vikurvanti dēvāḥ saṁsāramōcanam || 15 ||
contentBlocks.translation: are lifted out of saṁsāra by the God who is tender toward those who seek refuge. Likewise other gods also work liberation from saṁsāra. (15)
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रपन्नवत्सलो (prapannavatsalo) – affectionate to devotees (प्रपन्न:devotee | वत्सल:affectionate); देवः (devaḥ) – god; संसारात्तान्समुद्धरेत् (saṁsārāttānsamuddharet) – lifts them from the world (संसारात्:from the world | तान्:them | समुद्धरेत्:lifts); एवमन्ये (evamanye) – thus others (एवम्:thus | अन्ये:others); विकुर्वन्ति (vikurvanti) – perform; देवाः (devāḥ) – gods; संसारमोचनम् (saṁsāramocanam) – liberation from the world (संसार:world | मोचनम्:liberation);

contentBlocks.original:
मनुष्याणामृते देवं नान्या शक्तिस्तपोबलम् ।
इति तेनेन्द्रकल्पेन भगवान् सदसत्पतिः ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
manuṣyāṇāmr̥tē dēvaṁ nānyā śaktistapōbalam |
iti tēnēndrakalpēna bhagavān sadasatpatiḥ || 16 ||
contentBlocks.translation: But without that God, there is no other power of austerity among human beings. Thus by Indra-like splendour, the Blessed Lord who is the master of the real and unreal, (16)
contentBlocks.meanings: मनुष्याणामृते (manuṣyāṇāmṛte) – except for humans (मनुष्याणाम्:of humans | ऋते:except); देवं (devaṁ) – god; नान्या (nānyā) – no other; शक्तिस्तपोबलम् (śaktistapobalam) – power of austerity (शक्ति:power | तपो:austerity | बलम्:strength); इति (iti) – thus; तेनेन्द्रकल्पेन (tenendrakalpena) – by the Indra-like (तेन:by him | इन्द्रकल्पेन:Indra-like); भगवान् (bhagavān) – the Lord; सदसत्पतिः (sadasatpatiḥ) – lord of real and unreal (सत्:real | असत्:unreal | पतिः:lord);

contentBlocks.original:
कृत्तिवासाः स्तुतः कृष्ण तण्डिना शुभबुद्धिना ।
स्तवमेतं भगवतो ब्रह्मा स्वयमधारयत् ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kr̥ttivāsāḥ stutaḥ kr̥ṣṇa taṇḍinā śubhabuddhinā |
stavamētaṁ bhagavatō brahmā svayamadhārayat || 17 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Kṛttivāsā (Śiva, robed in elephant hide) was praised by Taṇḍi of pure mind, O Kṛṣṇa. Brahmā himself memorised this hymn to the Blessed One. (17)
contentBlocks.meanings: कृत्तिवासाः (kṛttivāsāḥ) – wearer of skins; स्तुतः (stutaḥ) – praised; कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa) – Krishna; तण्डिना (taṇḍinā) – by Taṇḍin; शुभबुद्धिना (śubhabuddhinā) – by auspicious-minded; स्तवमेतं (stavam etaṁ) – this hymn (स्तवम्:hymn | एतं:this); भगवतो (bhagavato) – of the Lord; ब्रह्मा (brahmā) – Brahma; स्वयमधारयत् (svayam adhārayat) – personally upheld (स्वयम्:personally | अधारयत्:upheld);

contentBlocks.original:
गीयते च स बुद्ध्येत ब्रह्मा शङ्करसन्निधौ ।
इदं पुण्यं पवित्रं च सर्वदा पापनाशनम् ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gīyatē ca sa buddhyēta brahmā śaṅkarasannidhau |
idaṁ puṇyaṁ pavitraṁ ca sarvadā pāpanāśanam || 18 ||
contentBlocks.translation: And by studying it, one understands; Brahmā sang it in the presence of Śaṅkara. This hymn is holy and purifying, ever the destroyer of sin. (18)
contentBlocks.meanings: गीयते (gīyate) – is sung; च (ca) – and; स (sa) – he; बुद्ध्येत (buddhyeta) – understands; ब्रह्मा (brahmā) – Brahma; शङ्करसन्निधौ (śaṅkarasannidhau) – in the presence of Shankara (शङ्कर:Shankara | सन्निधौ:in the presence); इदं (idaṁ) – this; पुण्यं (puṇyaṁ) – holy; पवित्रं (pavitraṁ) – pure; च (ca) – and; सर्वदा (sarvadā) – always; पापनाशनम् (pāpanāśanam) – destroyer of sins (पाप:sin | नाशनम्:destroyer);

contentBlocks.original:
योगदं मोक्षदं चैव स्वर्गदं तोषदं तथा ।
एवमेतत्पठन्ते य एकभक्त्या तु शङ्करम् ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgadaṁ mōkṣadaṁ caiva svargadaṁ tōṣadaṁ tathā |
ēvamētatpaṭhantē ya ēkabhaktyā tu śaṅkaram || 19 ||
contentBlocks.translation: It bestows yoga, liberation, heaven and satisfaction. Those who thus recite it with single-minded devotion to Śaṅkara — (19)
contentBlocks.meanings: योगदं (yogadaṁ) – giver of yoga (योग:yoga | दं:giver); मोक्षदं (mokṣadaṁ) – giver of liberation (मोक्ष:liberation | दं:giver); चैव (caiva) – and indeed; स्वर्गदं (svargadaṁ) – giver of heaven (स्वर्ग:heaven | दं:giver); तोषदं (toṣadaṁ) – giver of satisfaction (तोष:satisfaction | दं:giver); तथा (tathā) – thus; एवमेतत्पठन्ते (evametatpaṭhante) – thus they recite (एवम्:thus | एतत्:this | पठन्ते:recite); य (ya) – who; एकभक्त्या (ekabhaktyā) – with single devotion (एक:single | भक्त्या:devotion); तु (tu) – but; शङ्करम् (śaṅkaram) – Shankara;

contentBlocks.original:
या गतिः साङ्ख्ययोगानां व्रजन्त्येतां गतिं तदा ।
स्तवमेतं प्रयत्नेन सदा रुद्रस्य सन्निधौ ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yā gatiḥ sāṅkhyayōgānāṁ vrajantyētāṁ gatiṁ tadā |
stavamētaṁ prayatnēna sadā rudrasya sannidhau || 20 ||
contentBlocks.translation: they attain the same goal as the followers of Sāṁkhya yoga. Reciting this hymn in the presence of Rudra with great care (20)
contentBlocks.meanings: या (yā) – which; गतिः (gatiḥ) – path; साङ्ख्ययोगानां (sāṅkhyayogānāṁ) – of Sāṅkhya and Yoga (साङ्ख्य:Sāṅkhya | योगानां:of Yoga); व्रजन्त्येतां (vrajantyetāṁ) – attain this; गतिं (gatiṁ) – path; तदा (tadā) – then; स्तवमेतं (stavametaṁ) – this hymn (स्तवम्:hymn | एतं:this); प्रयत्नेन (prayatnena) – with effort; सदा (sadā) – always; रुद्रस्य (rudrasya) – of Rudra; सन्निधौ (sannidhau) – in the presence;

contentBlocks.original:
अब्दमेकं चरेद्भक्तः प्राप्नुयादीप्सितं फलम् ।
एतद्रहस्यं परमं ब्रह्मणो हृदि संस्थितम् ॥ २१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
abdamēkaṁ carēdbhaktaḥ prāpnuyādīpsitaṁ phalam |
ētadrahasyaṁ paramaṁ brahmaṇō hr̥di saṁsthitam || 21 ||
contentBlocks.translation: and practising it for a full year, the devoted one obtains the desired fruit. This supreme secret rests in the heart of Brahmā. (21)
contentBlocks.meanings: अब्दमेकं (abdam ekaṁ) – one year (अब्दम्:year | एकं:one); चरेद्भक्तः (cared bhaktaḥ) – devotee should practice (चरेत्:should practice | भक्तः:devotee); प्राप्नुयादीप्सितं (prāpnuyād īpsitaṁ) – would obtain desired (प्राप्नुयात्:would obtain | ईप्सितं:desired); फलम् (phalam) – fruit; एतद्रहस्यं (etad rahasyaṁ) – this secret (एतत्:this | रहस्यं:secret); परमं (paramaṁ) – supreme; ब्रह्मणो (brahmaṇo) – of Brahman; हृदि (hṛdi) – in the heart; संस्थितम् (saṁsthitam) – established;

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मा प्रोवाच शक्राय शक्रः प्रोवाच मृत्यवे ।
मृत्युः प्रोवाच रुद्रेभ्यो रुद्रेभ्यस्तण्डिमागमत् ॥ २२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmā prōvāca śakrāya śakraḥ prōvāca mr̥tyavē |
mr̥tyuḥ prōvāca rudrēbhyō rudrēbhyastaṇḍimāgamat || 22 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Brahmā transmitted it to Śakra (Indra); Śakra transmitted it to Mṛtyu (Death); Mṛtyu transmitted it to the Rudras; from the Rudras it reached Taṇḍi. (22)
contentBlocks.meanings: ब्रह्मा (brahmā) – Brahma; प्रोवाच (provāca) – said; शक्राय (śakrāya) – to Indra; शक्रः (śakraḥ) – Indra; प्रोवाच (provāca) – said; मृत्यवे (mṛtyave) – to Death; मृत्युः (mṛtyuḥ) – Death; प्रोवाच (provāca) – said; रुद्रेभ्यो (rudrebhyo) – to the Rudras; रुद्रेभ्यस्तण्डिमागमत् (rudrebhyastaṇḍimāgamat) – Tandi came to the Rudras (रुद्रेभ्यः:to the Rudras | तण्डि:Tandi | आगमत्:came);

contentBlocks.original:
महता तपसा प्राप्तस्तण्डिना ब्रह्मसद्मनि ।
तण्डिः प्रोवाच शुक्राय गौतमाय च भार्गवः ॥ २३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahatā tapasā prāptastaṇḍinā brahmasadmani |
taṇḍiḥ prōvāca śukrāya gautamāya ca bhārgavaḥ || 23 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Taṇḍi, having obtained it through great austerity in the abode of Brahmā, Bhārgava (Taṇḍi) transmitted it to Śukra and to Gautama. (23)
contentBlocks.meanings: महता (mahatā) – by great; तपसा (tapasā) – penance; प्राप्तस्तण्डिना (prāptastaṇḍinā) – obtained by Taṇḍin (प्राप्त:obtained | तण्डिना:by Taṇḍin); ब्रह्मसद्मनि (brahmasadmani) – in the abode of Brahma (ब्रह्म:Brahma | सद्मनि:in the abode); तण्डिः (taṇḍiḥ) – Taṇḍi; प्रोवाच (provāca) – said; शुक्राय (śukrāya) – to Śukra; गौतमाय (gautamāya) – to Gautama; च (ca) – and; भार्गवः (bhārgavaḥ) – Bhārgava;

contentBlocks.original:
वैवस्वताय मनवे गौतमः प्राह माधव ।
नारायणाय साध्याय समाधिष्ठाय धीमते ॥ २४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaivasvatāya manavē gautamaḥ prāha mādhava |
nārāyaṇāya sādhyāya samādhiṣṭhāya dhīmatē || 24 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Gautama transmitted it to Vaivasvata Manu. O Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), he told it to the wise Sādhya Nārāyaṇa who was absorbed in samādhi. (24)
contentBlocks.meanings: वैवस्वताय (vaivasvatāya) – to Vaivasvata; मनवे (manave) – to Manu; गौतमः (gautamaḥ) – Gautama; प्राह (prāha) – said; माधव (mādhava) – Madhava; नारायणाय (nārāyaṇāya) – to Nārāyaṇa; साध्याय (sādhyāya) – to the accomplished; समाधिष्ठाय (samādhiṣṭhāya) – to the meditative; धीमते (dhīmate) – to the wise;

contentBlocks.original:
यमाय प्राह भगवान् साध्यो नारायणोऽच्युतः ।
नाचिकेताय भगवानाह वैवस्वतो यमः ॥ २५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yamāya prāha bhagavān sādhyō nārāyaṇō:’cyutaḥ |
nācikētāya bhagavānāha vaivasvatō yamaḥ || 25 ||
contentBlocks.translation: The Blessed Sādhya Nārāyaṇa Acyuta transmitted it to Yama. The Blessed Vaivasvata Yama transmitted it to Nāciketa. (25)
contentBlocks.meanings: यमाय (yamāya) – to Yama; प्राह (prāha) – said; भगवान् (bhagavān) – the Lord; साध्यो (sādhyo) – Sādhyas; नारायणोऽच्युतः (nārāyaṇo'cyutaḥ) – Narayana, the infallible (नारायण:Narayana | अच्युतः:the infallible); नाचिकेताय (nāciketāya) – to Nachiketa; भगवानाह (bhagavānāha) – the Lord said (भगवान्:the Lord | आह:said); वैवस्वतो (vaivasvato) – son of Vivasvan; यमः (yamaḥ) – Yama;

contentBlocks.original:
मार्कण्डेयाय वार्ष्णेय नाचिकेतोऽभ्यभाषत ।
मार्कण्डेयान्मया प्राप्तो नियमेन जनार्दन ॥ २६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mārkaṇḍēyāya vārṣṇēya nācikētō:’bhyabhāṣata |
mārkaṇḍēyānmayā prāptō niyamēna janārdana || 26 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Nāciketa transmitted it to Mārkaṇḍeya, O Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa). From Mārkaṇḍeya I obtained it through discipline, O Janārdana. (26)
contentBlocks.meanings: मार्कण्डेयाय (mārkaṇḍeyāya) – to Mārkaṇḍeya; वार्ष्णेय (vārṣṇeya) – descendant of Vṛṣṇi; नाचिकेतोऽभ्यभाषत (nāciketo'bhyabhāṣata) – Naciketa spoke; मार्कण्डेयान्मया (mārkaṇḍeyānmayā) – from Mārkaṇḍeya by me (मार्कण्डेयान्:from Mārkaṇḍeya | मया:by me); प्राप्तो (prāpto) – obtained; नियमेन (niyamena) – by discipline; जनार्दन (janārdana) – Janardana;

contentBlocks.original:
तवाप्यहममित्रघ्न स्तवं दद्यां ह्यविश्रुतम् ।
स्वर्ग्यमारोग्यमायुष्यं धन्यं वेदेन सम्मितम् ॥ २७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tavāpyahamamitraghna stavaṁ dadyāṁ hyaviśrutam |
svargyamārōgyamāyuṣyaṁ dhanyaṁ vēdēna sammitam || 27 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Now I, O slayer of enemies, give you this hymn that has not been widely proclaimed. It grants heaven, health, long life and prosperity, equal in weight to the Vedas. (27)
contentBlocks.meanings: तवाप्यहममित्रघ्न (tavāpyahamamitraghna) – your enemy-destroyer (तव:your | अपि:also | अहम्:I | मित्रघ्न:enemy-destroyer); स्तवं (stavaṁ) – praise; दद्यां (dadyāṁ) – I would give; ह्यविश्रुतम् (hyaviśrutam) – indeed famous; स्वर्ग्यमारोग्यमायुष्यं (svargyamārogyamāyuṣyaṁ) – heavenly, healthy, long life (स्वर्ग्य:heavenly | आरोग्य:healthy | आयुष्य:long life); धन्यं (dhanyaṁ) – blessed; वेदेन (vedena) – by the Veda; सम्मितम् (sammitam) – agreed;

contentBlocks.original:
नास्य विघ्नं विकुर्वन्ति दानवा यक्षराक्षसाः ।
पिशाचा यातुधाना वा गुह्यका भुजगा अपि ॥ २८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nāsya vighnaṁ vikurvanti dānavā yakṣarākṣasāḥ |
piśācā yātudhānā vā guhyakā bhujagā api || 28 ||
contentBlocks.translation: Neither demons, yakṣas nor rākṣasas obstruct the reciter, nor do piśācas, yātudhānas, guhyakas or serpents cause any hindrance. (28)
contentBlocks.meanings: नास्य (nāsya) – not his; विघ्नं (vighnaṁ) – obstacle; विकुर्वन्ति (vikurvanti) – create; दानवा (dānavā) – demons; यक्षराक्षसाः (yakṣarākṣasāḥ) – yakṣas and rākṣasas (यक्ष:yakṣa | राक्षसाः:rākṣasas); पिशाचा (piśācā) – goblins; यातुधाना (yātudhānā) – evil spirits; वा (vā) – or; गुह्यका (guhyakā) – hidden beings; भुजगा (bhujagā) – serpents; अपि (api) – also;

contentBlocks.original:
यः पठेत शुचिः पार्थ ब्रह्मचारी जितेन्द्रियः । [भूत्वा]
अभग्नयोगो वर्षं तु सोऽश्वमेधफलं लभेत् ॥ २९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaḥ paṭhēta śuciḥ pārtha brahmacārī jitēndriyaḥ | [bhūtvā]
abhagnayōgō varṣaṁ tu sō:’śvamēdhaphalaṁ labhēt || 29 ||
contentBlocks.translation: One who is pure, celibate, self-controlled and of unbroken practice, O Pārtha, who recites this hymn diligently for one full year, obtains the fruit of the Aśvamedha sacrifice. (29)
contentBlocks.meanings: यः (yaḥ) – who; पठेत (paṭhet) – should recite; शुचिः (śuciḥ) – pure; पार्थ (pārtha) – O son of Pritha; ब्रह्मचारी (brahmacārī) – celibate; जितेन्द्रियः (jitendriyaḥ) – one who has controlled senses; अभग्नयोगो (abhagnayogaḥ) – unbroken in yoga (अभग्न:unbroken | योगः:yoga); वर्षं (varṣam) – year; तु (tu) – but; सोऽश्वमेधफलं (so'śvamedhaphalaṁ) – fruit of Ashvamedha (सः:he | अश्वमेध:Ashvamedha | फलं:fruit); लभेत् (labhet) – would obtain;

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीमहाभारते अनुशासनपर्वणि महादेवसहस्रनाम स्तोत्रं नाम सप्तदशोऽध्यायः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīmahābhāratē anuśāsanaparvaṇi mahādēvasahasranāma stōtraṁ nāma saptadaśō:’dhyāyaḥ ||
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the seventeenth chapter, known as the Mahādeva Sahasranāma Stotra, in the Anuśāsana Parva of the glorious Mahābhārata.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; श्रीमहाभारते (śrīmahābhārate) – in the revered Mahabharata (श्री:revered | महाभारते:Mahabharata); अनुशासनपर्वणि (anuśāsanaparvaṇi) – in the Anushasana Parva (अनुशासन:Anushasana | पर्वणि:in the section); महादेवसहस्रनाम (mahādevasahasranāma) – thousand names of Mahadeva (महादेव:Mahadeva | सहस्रनाम:thousand names); स्तोत्रं (stotraṁ) – hymn; नाम (nāma) – named; सप्तदशोऽध्यायः (saptadaśo'dhyāyaḥ) – seventeenth chapter (सप्तदश:seventeenth | अध्यायः:chapter);
